context.description: A philosophical discussion explains consciousness as the mechanism that turns perception into lived reality and the self, using a projection and light metaphor. It contrasts conscious and automatic breathing to show how awareness defines what enters experience and what can be remembered.
normalization_notes: Mapped raw category and subcategory to the closest supported body-content theoretical-discussion classification.
Translated the German section heading into English for retrieval normalization.

fields.section_heading: What the light shines on is

rows.paragraph_text: Consciousness is presented as the light that turns the world into lived reality, with awareness determining what becomes part of experience and the self. The text uses breathing to illustrate how conscious control can be taken on, but not consciously relinquished, except by shifting attention and forgetting.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2. Philosophical discussion of consciousness, awareness, reality, memory, and breathing. Section heading: What the light shines on is.
